channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: LCK Coach Faces Disciplinary Action after Sexual Reference in Post-Match Interview
item.content:encoded:
League of Legends Dplus Kia Head Coach, Kim ‘cvMax’ Dae-ho, has been reprimanded for comments made in the post-match interviews that followed Dplus Kia’s elimination from the LCK’s Road to MSI against KT Rolster.

Dplus Kia faced KT Rolster in the Road to MSI, falling to a 3-2 defeat, suffering a reverse sweep after blowing a 10k gold lead on Game 4 of the series

cvMax’s punishment regards a poist-match interview question regarding how it felt to lose that 10k gold lead. The coach replied, “It feels like when s**t abruptly stops even after only having done foreplay” (translated)

Those comments have seemingly sparked the disciplinary action that followed, with the Korean organization making a statement early on Monday that noted disciplinary action against the coach, as well as reinforced “communication and professional conduct training.”

Dplus Kia provided the following statement regarding the disciplinary action:

“We sincerely apologize for the concern caused to fans and members of the esports industry due to the inappropriate language used by Head Coach ‘cvMax’ Kim Dae-ho during a recent media interview following the Road to MSI match.

Taking this matter very seriously, we deeply recognize the importance of appropriate language expression and the accompanying social responsibility when communicating with the media and our fans.

Head Coach ‘cvMax’ Kim Dae-ho fully recognizes the seriousness of this matter and is deeply reflecting on his words and his actions.

To prevent similar incidents from occurring in the future, we will immediately implement the following measures.

We will comprehensively review and strengthen our internal media communication guidelines, establishing and strictly enforcing clear standards for public statements and media interviews.

We will take appropriate internal disciplinary measures regarding the head coach and reinforce communication and professional conduct training to demonstrate meaningful improvement.

In addition, we will implement regular professional training on responsible media engagement and effective communication practices, including the use of personal social media channels.”

cvMax returns to the spotlight after Siwoo controversy

The incident is not the first time this split that cvMax has been under the microscope for his conduct. The 35-year-old was criticized by several members of the League of Legends community, including Marc ‘Caedrel’ Lamont, for a post-game debrief that was shared on DK’s YouTube channel.

Again, following a loss against KT Rolster, the coach heavily criticized a visibly upset Jeon ‘Siwoo’ Si-woo, remarking, “You are the ace. The ace of making us lose.” Later adding, “Nobody played worse than you, honestly.” (translated)

Caedrel described the interaction as demotivating; however, other commenters, such as former Los Ratones Head Coach, Jakob ‘YamatoCannon’ Mebdi, described the interaction as normal for the region.

Let us know your thoughts on Insider Gaming’s Discord!
For other news, see the details of Insider Gaming’s Showcase!

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/cvmax-lck-road-to-msi-disciplinary-action/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1093)—Answers for Monday, June 8
item.content:encoded:
It’s time to add another notch onto that NYT Connections streak. In this guide, you’ll find the NYT Connections hints and solutions you need to power through the puzzle and come out the other side without too much fuss. If you’re stuck on today’s NYT Connections challenge, simply refer to the hints below!

Scroll down to get the NYT Connections answers you need!

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (June 8)

Here are the words for today’s NYT Connections:

  • INVISIBLE
  • MOHAWK
  • ISLAND
  • PUNCH
  • VOLLEYBALL
  • ELEPHANT
  • COCONUT
  • DELTA
  • PENINSULA
  • PATE
  • SEA URCHIN
  • MELON
  • OMEGA
  • ISTHMUS
  • DOME
  • RUNNING

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Parcels of land formed by water
  • Green – Slang terms for ‘head’
  • Blue – Things that can be spiked
  • Purple – Movies that have ‘Man’ in the title

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, June 8!

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, June 8 (#1093)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – DELTA, ISLAND, ISTHMUS, PENINSULA
  • Green – COCONUT, DOME, MELON, PATE
  • Blue – MOHAWK, PUNCH, SEA URCHIN, VOLLEYBALL
  • Purple – ELEPHANT, INVISIBLE, OMEGA, RUNNING

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-connections-hints-solutions-june-8-1093/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Fans Left Disappointed with Fully Regional Matchups at Valorant Masters London
item.content:encoded:
What do you expect to see at an International Valorant event? Of course, you expect the best teams in Valorant’s four major regions to go toe-to-toe against each other, providing the ultimate test to each team and each region.

Instead, Sunday’s Round 2 draw served up strictly regional matchups for the next few days of action, with teams only facing opposition from their home region in the Swiss bracket.

VCT Masters London Round 2 matches are strictly regional

The London Valorant Masters matchups for June 8 are the upper-bracket Swiss contests:

  • Vitality vs FUT (16:00 UTC/15:00 BST/10:00 EST)
  • NRG vs Leviatán (19:00 UTC/18:00 BST/13:00 EST)

The London Valorant Masters matchups for June 9 are the lower-bracket Swiss contests:

  • Dragon Ranger Gaming vs Xi Lai Gaming (16:00 UTC/15:00 BST/10:00 EST)
  • Global Esports vs Full Sense (19:00 UTC/18:00 BST/13:00 EST)

The lower bracket matches specifically mean that regional team-kills are guaranteed in the matchups against the Chinese and Pacific rosters, with the loser of each contest going home.

Valorant’s second international of the year has promised that fans will “witness new rivalries,” but they will have to wait for those matchups later in the event.

Ultimately, the draw created two interlinked suboptimal outcomes for fans: they will see the same regional matches they have been watching in VCT Stage 1, and they will see their own region hurt itself in its confusion.

The matchups drew jokes and comments from organizations and fans alike, with Vitality sharing a meme regarding their matchup with FUT. In the replies, event and draw host, Frankie Ward jokingly apologized to the French organization for the outcome of the draw.

Some fans called for more serious intervention, such as repeating the draw or the introduction of rules to forbid regional matchups at this stage, but ultimately, fans will just have to grin and bear it for one round of action.

The draw may have been a bit of bad luck, but at the end of the day, it guarantees at least one win for each region, so fans can be happy about that.

Let us know your Valorant Masters London Pick’ems predictions on Insider Gaming’s Discord!
For other news, see the details of Insider Gaming’s Showcase!

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

item.title: XBOX Exclusivity Will Be Decided ‘Case-By-Case’
item.content:encoded:
Matt Booty, XBOX’s Chief Content Officer, has outlined the new plans regarding exclusivity on XBOX, as he’s revealed it’ll largely be judged on a ‘case-by-case’ basis moving forward.

Of course, this follows the breaking news from the 2026 XBOX Games Showcase that Gears of War: E-Day and Clockwork Revolution will be XBOX console exclusives, though the messaging still remains blurred when the likes of Halo Campaign Evolved and Fable are still coming to PlayStation 5. Thankfully, Matt Booty set out to clarify XBOX’s position on the matter.

The Complex Matter Of XBOX Exclusives

Speaking to Gamertag Radio, Booty outlined how exclusivity will be handled at XBOX, and there are a few deciding factors.

Firstly, he set the record straight that big multiplayer and live-service titles will continue to be multiplatform, so there should be no confusion on that front. Following that, he explained that if XBOX had previously promised a title would be multiplatform—think of the aforementioned titles like Halo and Fable—then they’ll ‘honor that promise.’

With those two curve balls out of the way, it was time to discuss the general strategy, and Booty mentioned that they’ll look to ‘make the right decision, not the fast decision,’ as the team will continue to think about the matter moving forward. Until then, a game’s exclusivity will be judged on a ‘case-by-case’ basis, with the intention being to announce a game’s platforms alongside the release date.

If you think you’re having déjà vu, you wouldn’t be wrong, as it feels like this was the sentiment a few years back when it was announced that certain XBOX titles would be making the jump to PlayStation, with the likes of Indiana Jones and Forza Horizon soon following suit.

Unlike Sony, it feels like it’s far less black and white with XBOX, and this interview with Booty arguably raises more questions than it answers: Will the next Halo be cross-platform, considering it typically features a heavy online component? What about future Call of Duty titles and Elder Scrolls 6? It seems unlikely Microsoft will give up the revenue from other platforms, so where do they draw the line?

Time will tell, and perhaps in a year or two, once XBOX has upheld prior cross-platform promises, we’ll have a clearer picture.

What stance do you have regarding exclusives on XBOX? Should they be harsher and make more games console-exclusive, or share the love and publish more on PlayStation? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about our Valor Mortis preview that pens it as a GOTY contender, as well as the sorry state of ARC Raiders’ player count.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For June 8, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, June 8, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Type of boat
  • Hint 2: Usually transports passengers, sometimes vehicles
  • Hint 3: Runs on a regular schedule

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Railway
  • Island
  • Freight
  • Cruise
  • Taxi
  • Train
  • Harbor
  • Bus
  • Boat

Today’s Contexto Answer For June 8, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Ferry

A Ferry is a boat or ship designed to transport passengers, vehicles, and cargo across a body of water, typically on a regular service.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on June 9.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion
